special_requirements.comment: Casts were performed, fulfilling the requirement, though amplitude deductions were taken for not reaching true horizontal.
Tuck Flyaway was performed as the dismount.

artistry.notes: Overall body tension is present, but consistent minor form breaks (bent knees, flexed feet) detract from the quality of movement and extension. Rhythm was slightly broken by low casts.

coaching_summary: This routine demonstrates a solid foundation of Level 6 skills with good effort. The gymnast maintains a generally consistent pace and completes all required elements. However, consistent minor form breaks, particularly in leg and foot tension, and insufficient cast amplitude are significant areas for improvement. The dismount, while completed, shows clear technical faults that limit its scoring potential. Focusing on precise body lines and full extension will elevate the overall presentation and score.
top_3_fixes: **Cast Amplitude & Body Line:** Focus on aggressive hollow-arch-hollow swings to achieve true horizontal on all casts. Drill casts to handstand on a single bar with a spot to reinforce the vertical line and full hip extension. This is the 'Technical Anchor' that will be a liability at higher levels.

**Leg & Foot Tension (TPM/KTM):** Implement daily drills for pointed toes and locked knees in all positions – swings, kips, and circles. Use resistance bands for toe point drills and emphasize 'straight legs, tight knees' verbally and physically throughout training.

**Flyaway Dismount Shape:** Prioritize a strong, stretched hollow body position off the bar before initiating the tuck. Use a pit or soft landing surface to practice a 'straight body drop' before tucking, ensuring the rotation is initiated from a fully extended position, not an early pike. This will improve height and rotation quality.
celebrations: The gymnast successfully completed all required elements for a Level 6 routine.
The Squat On to High Bar was performed successfully, fulfilling the B-value requirement.
The routine maintained a generally good pace and flow between most skills.
